title: Kuma’s Life as a Buccaneer
description: On pages 14–16, this part focuses on Unknown person #13, Kuma, Ivankov, Stussy, Saturn, Unknown person #14 as Bonney discovers Kuma’s memories, revealing his Buccaneer heritage, Ginny, the Revolutionary Army, God Valley, and the painful origins behind Kuma’s sacrifice.
scene: Bonney discovers Kuma’s memories, revealing his Buccaneer heritage, Ginny, the Revolutionary Army, God Valley, and the painful origins behind Kuma’s sacrifice.

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: To be more precise, I'm going to perform a stem cell transplant.

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: With the amount of money and effort involved in the procedure, it'll be like making a cyborg!!

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #13
dialogue.text: You're gonna turn her into a cyborg?!

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #13
dialogue.text: No, no! I'm just comparing the cost.

dialogue.speaker: Kuma
dialogue.text: Are you a buccaneer?!

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #13
dialogue.text: Yeah.

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: You know I work for the government, right?!

dialogue.speaker: Ivankov
dialogue.text: ...I trust Dragon.

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #13
dialogue.text: What makes my blood so special anyway?

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: I don't have enough samples to explain it in any detail...

dialogue.speaker: Stussy
dialogue.text: You want to clone me?

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: That's right!! I've been searching for the perfect model for my clone soldier project!!

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: If you donate some blood and give me permission to make your clones into soldiers...

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: ...I'll wave all the medical expenses for Bonney's procedure.

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #13
dialogue.text: You don't have any money, I assume?

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: Well, it's not like I can pass on an offer like that anyway...

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: But what are you going to do with them?

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: An army where all the soldiers look just like me... Are they going to be like the marines?

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: That's the gist of it! Have you heard of electromagnetic energy?

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: My soldiers will have bodies so sturdy that they deflect bullets.

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: They'll be heroes that protect the weak and helpless!!

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: If only I could get them to fire lasers too, those pirates would be scared speechless!!

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: In the face of devastating scientific power, evildoers have no choice but to submit!!

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: They'll be warriors of the future. There's no limit to their potential!!

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: He said you were open about your ambitions... He was right about you being both eccentric and trustworthy.

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: Dragon said that, did he?! He's always been a bit holier than thou!!

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: If clones of me end up saving people someday...

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: Maybe I can view that as a good reason for why I was born.

dialogue.speaker: Ivankov
dialogue.text: DRAGON won't be happy about this arrangement...

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: It's okay... I have nothing but gratitude for you both.

dialogue.speaker: Saturn
dialogue.text: Honestly... If it'd cure Bonney,

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: I would've made a deal with the devil himself.

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #14
dialogue.text: Please, just save her.

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #14
dialogue.text: You're quite the saint.

dialogue.speaker: Celestial Dragons
dialogue.text: The Holy Land of Mariejois

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: The clone soldiers are meant to be "weagons."

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: A weapon's worth is defined by how many people it can kill.

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: Vegapunk is a fool for not making the most of this chance...

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #14
dialogue.text: No matter, I have my own ideas...!!

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #14
dialogue.text: Yes, sir! your word is our command!

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #14
dialogue.text: Me? A saint? Don't be silly!

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: I'm just a spineless pacifist. Of course I love keeping the peace.

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: 9e pe be be be be de! Keeping the peace, eh? I like the sound of that! Why don't we call them the PacificTA?!

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #14
dialogue.text: They only wanted peace....!!

summary: This part advances the Kuma and Bonney’s Tragedy through 44 cleaned dialogue beats, focusing on character decisions, battlefield momentum, and the story’s larger consequences.
